items.headline: Avdija Explodes for 35, Blazers Edge Clippers in Crucial Play-In Battle!
items.summary: Deni Avdija delivered a career-high 35 points (11-19 FG, 12-13 FT), along with 4 rebounds, 5 assists, and a block, to lead the POR past the LAC 116-97. The stellar performance gives Portland the inside track for the Western Conference's 8th seed heading into the final day of the regular season.

items.headline: Wemby's Historic Night: 40 Points, Award Eligibility Secured!
items.summary: San Antonio's rookie phenom Victor Wembanyama showcased his generational talent with 40 points, 13 rebounds, and 5 assists in just 26 minutes during the Spurs' 139-120 win over the DAL. This dominant outing marks his 65th game, officially making him eligible for end-of-season NBA awards.

items.headline: NBA Grapples with Widespread Load Management as Playoffs Loom
items.summary: A staggering 168 players were reportedly sidelined on Friday due to illness, injury, or rest, including recent MVPs Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Nikola Jokic. This trend highlights the league's ongoing challenge of balancing player health and competitive integrity as the regular season concludes.

items.headline: Trail Blazers secure vital win over Clippers
items.summary: Deni Avdija led Portland with 35 points in a crucial victory.

items.headline: Kings outduel Warriors in high-scoring affair
items.summary: Devin Carter's career-high 29 points powered Sacramento.

items.headline: Timberwolves hold off Rockets in close contest
items.summary: Amen Thompson erupted for a career-high 41 points for Houston in a losing effort.

items.headline: Hawks clinch playoff spot with dominant win over Cavaliers
items.summary: Dyson Daniels posted a triple-double as Atlanta secured its playoff berth.

items.headline: Lakers cruise past Suns, clinch home-court advantage
items.summary: LeBron James neared a triple-double and reached a historic assist milestone.

items.headline: Spurs dominate Mavericks behind Wembanyama's heroics
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama's 40-point effort led San Antonio to victory.

items.headline: Knicks overcome Magic in thrilling semifinal
items.summary: Jalen Brunson's 40 points propelled New York to the NBA Cup Final.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Explosive Performance
items.summary: Israeli star Deni Avdija dropped a career-high 35 points, shooting 11-of-19 from the field and 12-of-13 from the free-throw line, plus 5 assists and 4 rebounds in POR's critical win over LAC.

items.headline: Wembanyama Makes History
items.summary: Rookie Victor Wembanyama became the only player in NBA history to record 40 points, 13 rebounds, and 5 assists in under 30 minutes, doing so in just 26 minutes against DAL.

items.headline: Amen Thompson's Career Night
items.summary: HOU's rookie Amen Thompson poured in a career-high 41 points, adding 9 rebounds and 7 assists against MIN.

items.headline: LeBron Reaches Assist Milestone
items.summary: LAL's LeBron James tallied 28 points, 12 assists, and 6 rebounds in the victory over PHX, becoming just the fourth player in NBA history to eclipse 12,000 career assists.

items.headline: Dyson Daniels' Second Triple-Double
items.summary: ATL's Dyson Daniels posted his second career triple-double with 13 points, 10 rebounds, and 12 assists in the Hawks' win over CLE.

items.headline: Jalen Brunson's Playoff Heroics
items.summary: Jalen Brunson led the NYK to the NBA Cup Final with a sensational 40-point, 8-assist performance in their semifinal win against ORL.

items.headline: Playoff Picture Remains Hazy on Final Day
items.summary: The NBA enters its final regular season day with all playoff and play-in matchups still undecided. High-stakes games on Sunday will determine final seedings across both conferences.

items.headline: Doncic Out for Regular Season, Jokic Questionable for Awards Eligibility
items.summary: LAL star Luka Doncic is out for the remainder of the regular season with a Grade 2 left hamstring strain, impacting his award eligibility. DEN's Nikola Jokic is questionable for today's game and needs to play to meet the 65-game requirement for awards.

items.headline: Pierce Urges 76ers to Trade Embiid: 'Trust The Process Is Over'
items.summary: NBA legend Paul Pierce has publicly stated that the PHI should trade Joel Embiid, citing another injury-plagued season and suggesting it's time to build around Tyrese Maxey and VJ Edgecombe.

items.headline: Warriors' Bench Sparking Late-Season Surge
items.summary: The GSW have won 11 of their last 15 games, with strong contributions from bench players like De'Anthony Melton, Al Horford, and Brandin Podziemski proving crucial for their playoff push.
